title: Fee-on-Transfer Tokens Can Break Intent Accounting
Missing Destination Field Validation Allows Malformed Intents On-Chain
Missing Existence Check in remove_memo_mapping Emits Spurious Events
Unused Error Variants Reduce ABI Clarity
description:
Several error variants are defined but never returned:

- **Intent Bridge:** `Error::AlreadyInitialized` (1), `Error::InvalidDeadlineDuration` (11). The constructor uses `panic!()` instead of returning these errors.
- **Token Forwarder:** `Error::MemoNotFound` (5). Defined but unused.

**Recommendation:** Remove unused variants or wire them into the code paths (e.g., use `Error::InvalidDeadlineDuration` in the constructor with a `Result` return type instead of `panic!()`).

title: Outdated soroban-sdk Dependency Contains Three Known CVEs
description:
Both contracts pin `soroban-sdk = "22.0.0"`, which is affected by three published CVEs:

| CVE | CVSS | Issue | Fixed In |
|---|---|---|---|
| CVE-2026-24889 | Medium | `Bytes::slice`, `Vec::slice`, `Prng::gen_range` overflow | >= 22.0.9 |
| CVE-2026-26267 | High | `#[contractimpl]` resolves inherent over trait impl, bypassing auth | >= 22.0.10 |
| CVE-2026-32322 | Medium (5.3) | `Fr` scalar field equality bypasses modular reduction | >= 22.0.11 |

The vulnerable code paths are not exercised in current contracts, but the dependency ships known vulnerable code. Future modifications could inadvertently exercise affected APIs.

**Recommendation:** Upgrade to `soroban-sdk >= 22.0.11` and recompile/redeploy:

```toml
[dependencies]
soroban-sdk = "22.0.11"
```

title: Immutable Role Configuration Prevents On-Chain Key Rotation After Deployment
Missing Persistent TTL Extension on Memo Mappings Can Cause Silent Routing Failure
Missing Instance Storage TTL Extension Can Cause Temporary Contract Unavailability
